steps.title: Spot the defining contrast
steps.content: "While rearing by female-male pairs is most common, male-male pairs sometimes rear cygnets too, achieving a significantly higher rate of survival" — this contrast shows the family structure succeeds with multiple configurations, not just one.

steps.title: Match to the correct word
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean flexibility or adaptability — the passage shows the familial structure working across different pair types (female-male and male-male) and even fostering from other families. Substitute each choice:
  • "elasticity": means the capacity to adapt and function across varying conditions — directly captures how the black swan family structure succeeds in multiple configurations. ✓
  • "reciprocity": means mutual exchange or give-and-take between parties — describes a two-way relationship, not the structural flexibility across pair types shown in the passage. ✗
  • "cohesion": means unity or sticking together — describes how tightly a group bonds, not the ability to function in varied structural arrangements. ✗
  • "affection": means emotional warmth or fondness — describes a feeling between individuals, not an adaptive structural quality of the family unit. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
